extra.reasoning:
The property requires that flipping focal_weakness from false to true (all else equal) never lowers acuity. focal_weakness appears in only one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100), whose 12-term conjunction includes focal_weakness and requires all of {fever, sweating, back_pain, slurred_speech, abdominal_pain, dyspnea, chest_pain, dizziness, vision_loss, syncope, leg_swelling, focal_weakness} to be true; it sets acuity := self_care.

Consider the input pair where those other 11 conjunction members are all true and vomiting is anything (here false).

BEFORE (focal_weakness=false): interaction_drop does not fire because focal_weakness is false. Multiple emergent rules fire — raise_sweating (p3), raise_slurred_speech (p3), raise_dyspnea (p3), raise_dizziness (p3), raise_syncope (p3) — alongside several priority-2 urgent rules. The highest-priority matching rule is priority 3 → acuity = emergent.

AFTER (focal_weakness=true): now all 12 conjuncts hold, so interaction_drop fires at priority 100, beating every priority-3 emergent rule → acuity = self_care.

Setting focal_weakness true changes acuity from emergent to self_care — a strict decrease in the ordered enum [self_care < routine < urgent < emergent]. This violates monotonicity. The witness is the focal_weakness=true assignment (vomiting=false), which yields self_care, while its focal_weakness=false counterpart yields emergent.
